Acknowledgment

Congratulations on Completing Your Application, Jorge!

Welcome to Student Orientation

As a member of the American Public University System community, you have certain rights and responsibilities that are essential to maintaining an environment conducive to learning and personal growth.

This orientation will cover:

  • Academic Policies - Understanding academic integrity, grading policies, and student academic rights
  • Student Code of Conduct - Expected behavior and community standards
  • Privacy Rights (FERPA) - How your educational records are protected
  • Your Acknowledgment - Confirming your understanding and agreement

Note: You must complete all items in the student orientation before proceeding.

Estimated time to complete orientation: 15-30 minutes

Contact Your Enrollment Advisor

Contact your dedicated enrollment advisor today if you get stuck. They are here to support you every step of the way.

S

Mon-Fri 8am-8pm EST

Academic Policies

Academic Integrity

APUS is committed to maintaining the highest standards of academic integrity. All students are expected to:

  • Submit original work and properly cite all sources
  • Avoid plagiarism, cheating, and unauthorized collaboration
  • Uphold honest and ethical academic practices in all coursework
  • Report suspected violations of academic integrity
Violations of academic integrity may result in course failure, suspension, or dismissal from the university.

Grading and Assessment

Students have the right to:

  • Receive clear grading criteria and assignment expectations
  • Timely feedback on assignments and assessments
  • Appeal grades through the proper academic channels
  • Access course materials and resources necessary for success

Attendance and Participation

Regular participation in online coursework is required. Students are expected to:

  • Log in to their courses regularly (at least 3 times per week recommended)
  • Complete assignments by posted deadlines
  • Participate in discussion forums and collaborative activities
  • Communicate with instructors regarding absences or extensions

Student Orientation Checklist

Complete all items below to proceed:

0 of 7 completed

Student Code of Conduct

Community Standards

As a member of the APUS community, you are expected to:

  • Treat all community members with respect and dignity
  • Engage in civil discourse, even when opinions differ
  • Refrain from harassment, discrimination, or intimidation
  • Use university resources responsibly and ethically
  • Comply with all federal, state, and local laws

Prohibited Conduct

The following behaviors are strictly prohibited:

  • Academic dishonesty in any form
  • Harassment or discrimination based on protected characteristics
  • Threats, violence, or intimidation of any kind
  • Disruption of university operations or learning environment
  • Unauthorized access to university systems or data
  • Misuse of university technology or resources

Disciplinary Procedures

Students accused of policy violations have the right to:

  • Written notice of alleged violations
  • A fair and impartial hearing process
  • Present evidence and witnesses on their behalf
  • Appeal decisions through established procedures

Sanctions for violations may include warning, probation, suspension, or dismissal, depending on the severity of the infraction.

Privacy Rights - FERPA

What is FERPA?

The Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act (FERPA) is a federal law that protects the privacy of your education records. APUS is committed to protecting your privacy in accordance with FERPA regulations.

Your Rights Under FERPA

You have the right to:

  • Inspect and Review: Access your educational records within 45 days of a request
  • Request Amendment: Request corrections to records you believe are inaccurate or misleading
  • Consent to Disclosure: Control disclosure of personally identifiable information from your records
  • File Complaints: File a complaint with the U.S. Department of Education concerning alleged failures to comply with FERPA

Directory Information

APUS may disclose "directory information" without your consent unless you request otherwise. Directory information includes:

  • Name, address, telephone number, email address
  • Dates of attendance, enrollment status
  • Degrees, honors, and awards received
  • Most recent educational institution attended

You may request to withhold directory information by contacting the Registrar's Office.

When Information May Be Disclosed

APUS may disclose education records without consent to:

  • School officials with legitimate educational interest
  • Other schools to which you are transferring
  • Authorized representatives for audit or evaluation purposes
  • Parties in connection with financial aid
  • Organizations conducting studies for or on behalf of the school
  • Accrediting organizations
  • Comply with judicial orders or lawfully issued subpoenas
  • Appropriate officials in health or safety emergencies

Acknowledgment and Agreement

By checking the boxes below, you acknowledge that you have read, understood, and agree to abide by the policies outlined in this review.

Your acknowledgment will be recorded with a timestamp and associated with your student record.

Acknowledgment Complete!

Thank you for reviewing and acknowledging the APUS Student Rights and Responsibilities. Your acknowledgment has been recorded.
